item:advanced-knowledge.description: Advanced civic and technical knowledge produced by academies and used to unlock prospecting, mining, and later industrial plans.
item:advanced-knowledge.name: Advanced Knowledge

item:aquamarine.description: A polished cyan gemstone reserved for late-game treasure economy.
item:aquamarine.name: Aquamarine

item:axe.description: Single-use chopping tool. Drag onto tree sources to harvest logs; also still serves later expedition gear chains.
item:axe.name: Axe

item:basic-knowledge.description: Foundational know-how produced by schools and used to unlock early specialized plans.
item:basic-knowledge.name: Basic Knowledge

item:beer.description: A single mug of beer served by taverns.
item:beer.name: Beer

item:beer-barrel.description: A barrel of beer produced by breweries and served by taverns.
item:beer-barrel.name: Beer Barrel

item:bio-waste.description: Farm byproduct from animals and messy food chains. It belongs on the board until processed, because pretending manure fits neatly in a pocket is how UI crimes begin.
item:bio-waste.name: Bio Waste

item:bread.description: Baked food produced by bakeries from flour and water.
item:bread.name: Bread

item:bricks.description: Fired clay building material prepared for sturdier construction.
item:bricks.name: Bricks

item:building-permit.description: Official construction authorization produced by the Civic Office and used as the Era IV master item.
item:building-permit.name: Building Permit

item:charcoal.description: Controlled pre-industrial fuel made from logs and used by kilns and furnaces before coal mining is unlocked.
item:charcoal.name: Charcoal

item:cheat.description: A shameless little toolkit that opens the cheat inventory for testing.
item:cheat.label: Cheats
item:cheat.name: Cheat Kit

item:cheat:speed-disable.description: Cheat timepiece that switches the engine back to normal timing without confirmation.
item:cheat:speed-disable.name: Closed Onion Watch

item:cheat:speed-enable.description: Cheat timepiece that switches the engine into 1s instant timing mode without confirmation.
item:cheat:speed-enable.name: Open Onion Watch

item:cheese.description: Processed dairy food produced from milk and used by cookhouses and markets.
item:cheese.name: Cheese

item:chest-t1.description: Locked chest returned from a short Heroes Guild expedition. Open it with a Key I.
item:chest-t1.name: Chest I

item:chest-t2.description: Locked chest returned from a tougher Heroes Guild expedition. Open it with a Key II.
item:chest-t2.name: Chest II

item:chest-t3.description: Locked chest returned from a dangerous Heroes Guild expedition. Open it with a Key III.
item:chest-t3.name: Chest III

item:chest-t4.description: Locked chest returned from the longest Heroes Guild expedition. Open it with a Key IV.
item:chest-t4.name: Chest IV

item:clay.description: Raw clay prepared for brick and ceramic production.
item:clay.name: Clay

item:clay-deposit.description: Movable clay source used by nearby clay pits.
item:clay-deposit.name: Clay Deposit

item:coal.description: A mining cart loaded with coal. Used as fuel by smelters.
item:coal.name: Coal Cart

item:coal-deposit.description: Movable coal source used by nearby coal mines.
item:coal-deposit.name: Coal Deposit

item:coin.description: Basic currency produced by markets from processed food and drink, and later by specialist economy buildings.
item:coin.name: Coin

item:coin-stack.description: Minted high-value currency stack produced by the Goldsmith and used to fund advanced guilds, university work, and long expeditions.
item:coin-stack.name: Coin Stack

item:common-cloth.description: Plain woven cloth produced from wool by weaver huts.
item:common-cloth.name: Common Cloth

item:common-clothing.description: Practical clothing sewn from common cloth and leather.
item:common-clothing.name: Common Clothing

item:construction-bundle.description: Master package of fired bricks, glass, roof tiles, permits, and prestige materials used to unlock advanced civic and guild buildings.
item:construction-bundle.name: Construction Bundle

item:double-tree.description: Tier-2 watered wood source: two conifers packed together for stronger nearby lumberjack log rolls. Harvesting it with a single-use axe removes the source and drops logs.
item:double-tree.name: Double Tree

item:egg.description: Fresh eggs produced by chicken coops for base food supply chains.
item:egg.name: Egg

item:emerald.description: A polished green gemstone prepared for later treasure recipes.
item:emerald.name: Emerald

item:feast.description: Hearty processed food platter that proves Era III food production is complete.
item:feast.name: Feast

item:flour.description: Milled grain produced by windmills and baked into bread.
item:flour.name: Flour

item:food-supply.description: Packed base food supply prepared from grain, animal produce, and vegetables. Era II master item.
item:food-supply.name: Food Supply

item:glass.description: Finished glass panels prepared for advanced building and luxury chains.
item:glass.name: Glass

item:gold-deposit.description: Movable gold source used by nearby gold mines.
item:gold-deposit.name: Gold Deposit

item:gold-ingot.description: Refined gold produced by smelters from gold ore and coal.
item:gold-ingot.name: Gold Ingot

item:gold-ore.description: A mining cart loaded with gold ore. This is the high-value mining output that proves the settlement can reach deep mineral resources.
item:gold-ore.name: Gold Ore Cart

item:grain.description: Harvested grain produced by farms and milled into flour or used as pig feed.
item:grain.name: Grain

item:grapes.description: Harvested grapes produced by wineries when supplied with water near a vineyard.
item:grapes.name: Grapes

item:guild-charter.description: Formal civic charter that authorizes advanced guild, university, and expedition buildings after the city has advanced construction capacity.
item:guild-charter.name: Guild Charter

item:hop-field.description: Movable hop source used by nearby breweries.
item:hop-field.name: Hop Field

item:hops.description: Harvested hops produced by breweries when supplied with water near a hop field.
item:hops.name: Hops

item:inventory.description: A trusty backpack that opens the shared inventory from the board.
item:inventory.label: Inventory
item:inventory.name: Backpack

item:iron-armor.description: Heavy iron armor upgraded by the Armory from leather armor, iron ingots, nails, and charcoal-fired fittings.
item:iron-armor.name: Iron Armor

item:iron-deposit.description: Movable iron source used by nearby iron mines.
item:iron-deposit.name: Iron Deposit

item:iron-ingot.description: Refined iron produced by smelters from iron ore and coal.
item:iron-ingot.name: Iron Ingot

item:iron-ore.description: A mining cart loaded with iron ore. Smelters refine it into iron ingots.
item:iron-ore.name: Iron Ore Cart

item:key-t1.description: Basic goldsmith key used to open Chest I and bootstrap the gem/key expedition loop.
item:key-t1.name: Key I

item:key-t2.description: Gem-set goldsmith key used to open Chest II and fund tougher expeditions.
item:key-t2.name: Key II

item:key-t3.description: Advanced gem-set goldsmith key used to open Chest III and authorize grand expeditions.
item:key-t3.name: Key III

item:key-t4.description: Masterwork gem-set goldsmith key used to open Chest IV.
item:key-t4.name: Key IV

item:leather.description: Processed leather prepared by tanneries from raw hide and used by clothing and equipment chains.
item:leather.name: Leather

item:leather-armor.description: Layered leather armor assembled by the Armory from leather, clothing, and forged nails.
item:leather-armor.name: Leather Armor

item:log.description: Basic wood material produced by lumberjacks.
item:log.name: Log

item:luxury-cloth.description: Refined dyed cloth used for high-status clothing and later civic luxuries.
item:luxury-cloth.name: Luxury Cloth

item:luxury-clothing.description: Prestige clothing sewn from luxury cloth, leather, and coins.
item:luxury-clothing.name: Luxury Clothing

item:marble.description: Raw white marble extracted by Quarry II before being finished by Stonemason II.
item:marble.name: Marble

item:marble-block.description: Polished marble construction block for future prestige buildings that should not be made from yet another heroic pile of planks.
item:marble-block.name: Marble Block

item:marble-deposit.description: Movable premium stone source discovered by Prospector Guild 2 and used by Quarry II for luxury construction materials.
item:marble-deposit.name: Marble Deposit

item:master-knowledge.description: University-level scholarship used to authorize the most ambitious expeditions and future high-civic progression.
item:master-knowledge.name: Master Knowledge

item:micro-forest.description: Tier-3 tiny conifer forest. Nearby lumberjacks get the strongest log roll contribution from it. Harvesting it with a single-use axe removes the source and drops logs.
item:micro-forest.name: Micro-Forest

item:milk.description: Fresh milk produced by cattle farms for base food supply chains.
item:milk.name: Milk

item:morale-t1.description: Tier I social morale generated by housing and spent as an optional boost currency or prestige cost.
item:morale-t1.name: Morale I

item:morale-t2.description: Tier II social morale generated by housing and spent as an optional boost currency or prestige cost.
item:morale-t2.name: Morale II

item:morale-t3.description: Tier III social morale generated by housing and spent as an optional boost currency or prestige cost.
item:morale-t3.name: Morale III

item:morale-t4.description: Tier IV social morale generated by housing and spent as an optional boost currency or prestige cost.
item:morale-t4.name: Morale IV

item:nails.description: Forged fasteners produced by the Blacksmith and consumed by armories and later advanced construction.
item:nails.name: Nails

item:nuke-save.description: A dangerous reset charge that opens the save nuke confirmation.
item:nuke-save.label: Nuke
item:nuke-save.name: Save Nuke

item:paper.description: Processed writing material used by schools, civic offices, prospectors, and improved trade.
item:paper.name: Paper

item:pickaxe.description: Single-use mining tool. Drag onto a stone deposit to harvest a random pile of stones.
item:pickaxe.name: Pickaxe

item:piglet.description: A small piglet produced by pig farms and processed by slaughterhouses.
item:piglet.name: Piglet

item:pigment.description: Concentrated natural dye powders produced by Dye Workshops for luxury textiles and stained-glass work.
item:pigment.name: Pigment

item:plank.description: Processed wood material produced by sawmills.
item:plank.name: Plank

item:pollution.description: Dirty board-only industrial pollution emitted by coal or charcoal processing. It stays on the board and must be cleaned by Purifier I.
item:pollution.name: Pollution

item:raw-hide.description: Untreated animal hide produced by slaughterhouses and processed by tanneries into leather.
item:raw-hide.name: Raw Hide

item:rock.description: Movable stone deposit used by nearby quarries or mined manually with a single-use pickaxe.
item:rock.name: Stone Deposit

item:roof-tiles.description: Curved fired roof tiles prepared for higher-tier building construction.
item:roof-tiles.name: Roof Tiles

item:ruby.description: A polished red gemstone recovered from low-tier hero loot.
item:ruby.name: Ruby

item:sand.description: Fine sand prepared as the base material for glassmaking.
item:sand.name: Sand

item:sand-deposit.description: Movable sand source used by nearby sand pits.
item:sand-deposit.name: Sand Deposit

item:sapphire.description: A polished blue gemstone from better guild rewards.
item:sapphire.name: Sapphire

item:sausage.description: Meat food produced by slaughterhouses from piglets and consumed by heavy industry workers.
item:sausage.name: Sausage

item:stained-glass.description: Prestige colored glass panels finished by Glazier Workshops for later high-civic and luxury buildings.
item:stained-glass.name: Stained Glass

item:stone.description: Basic stone material produced by quarries.
item:stone.name: Stone

item:stone-block.description: Processed stone material produced by stonemasons.
item:stone-block.name: Stone Block

item:sword.description: Expedition weapon forged from iron ingots, leather grip work, and charcoal heat.
item:sword.name: Sword

item:topaz.description: A polished amber gemstone from high-tier treasure chests.
item:topaz.name: Topaz

item:trash.description: Generic board waste byproduct from messy production lines. It is harmless, ugly, and should eventually be cleaned instead of hidden away.
item:trash.name: Trash

item:treasure-chest.description: Master reward from a grand Heroes Guild expedition, packed with gold and gems and ready to gate the next civic tier.
item:treasure-chest.name: Treasure Chest

item:tree.description: Movable tier-1 wood source. Nearby lumberjacks use it for log drop rolls. Harvesting it with a single-use axe removes the source and drops logs.
item:tree.name: Tree

item:vegetables.description: Fresh vegetables grown in small gardens for base food supply chains.
item:vegetables.name: Vegetables

item:vineyard.description: Movable vineyard source used by nearby wineries.
item:vineyard.name: Vineyard

item:water.description: Basic water resource produced by wells and consumed by farms.
item:water.name: Water

item:wheat-field.description: Movable grain source used by nearby farms.
item:wheat-field.name: Wheat Field

item:wine-barrel.description: A barrel of wine produced by wineries and served by taverns.
item:wine-barrel.name: Wine Barrel

item:wine-glass.description: A single glass of wine served by taverns.
item:wine-glass.name: Wine Glass

item:wool.description: Raw wool produced by sheep pastures and used as early settlement material.
item:wool.name: Wool

producer:academy.description: Industrial education hub that converts civic knowledge into advanced knowledge and issues mining and metallurgy plans.
producer:academy.name: Academy

producer:armory-t1.description: Dedicated armor workshop that turns leather, clothing, nails, and iron ingots into expedition armor.
producer:armory-t1.name: Armory I

producer:bakery-t1.description: Consumes flour and water to bake bread.
producer:bakery-t1.name: Bakery I

producer:bio-waste-processor-t1.description: Early farm cleanup building that composts bio-waste safely or burns it faster with wood at the cost of pollution risk.
producer:bio-waste-processor-t1.label: I
producer:bio-waste-processor-t1.name: Bio-Waste Processor I

producer:bio-waste-processor-t2.description: Upgraded farm cleanup building that can also burn bio-waste with coal or charcoal when speed beats dignity.
producer:bio-waste-processor-t2.label: II
producer:bio-waste-processor-t2.name: Bio-Waste Processor II

producer:blacksmith-t1.description: Guild-era iron sink that forges nails, axes, and swords from iron ingots and charcoal.
producer:blacksmith-t1.name: Blacksmith I

producer:brewery-t1.description: Produces hops from water near a hop field, then brews hops and water into beer barrels.
producer:brewery-t1.name: Brewery I

producer:brickyard.description: Kiln workshop that fires clay into bricks for advanced construction.
producer:brickyard.name: Brickyard

producer:cathedral.description: Choose The Path keystone for the Faith branch. Owning it locks Engineers and Mages and prepares later devotion, cleansing, and order lines.
producer:cathedral.name: Cathedral

producer:cattle-farm-t1.description: Consumes grain and water to produce milk.
producer:cattle-farm-t1.name: Cattle Farm I

producer:charcoal-burner-t1.description: Dirty kiln workshop that turns logs into charcoal and must work near a purifier.
producer:charcoal-burner-t1.name: Charcoal Burner I

producer:chicken-coop-t1.description: Consumes grain and water to produce eggs.
producer:chicken-coop-t1.name: Chicken Coop I

producer:civic-office-t1.description: Administration producer that converts paper, knowledge, and coins into building permits.
producer:civic-office-t1.name: Civic Office I

producer:clay-pit.description: Prepared extraction pit that produces clay near a surveyed clay deposit.
producer:clay-pit.name: Clay Pit

producer:coal-mine-t1.description: Mines coal carts from a nearby coal deposit using food and water for the crew.
producer:coal-mine-t1.name: Coal Mine I

producer:construction-yard-t1.description: Advanced construction yard that bundles bricks, glass, roof tiles, permits, and civic goods into settlement expansion packages.
producer:construction-yard-t1.name: Construction Yard I

producer:dye-workshop-t1.description: Textile producer that dyes common cloth into luxury cloth.
producer:dye-workshop-t1.name: Dye Workshop I

producer:farm-t1.description: Consumes water and produces grain when placed near a wheat field.
producer:farm-t1.name: Farm I

producer:glassworks.description: Furnace workshop that turns sand and charcoal into glass.
producer:glassworks.name: Glassworks

producer:glazier-workshop-t1.description: Artisan glass finishing workshop that turns plain glass and pigment into stained glass for future prestige buildings.
producer:glazier-workshop-t1.name: Glazier Workshop I

producer:gold-mine-t1.description: Mines gold ore carts from a nearby gold deposit using richer provisions.
producer:gold-mine-t1.name: Gold Mine I

producer:goldsmith-t1.description: Guild-era gold sink that mints coin stacks and crafts gem-set keys for the expedition loop.
producer:goldsmith-t1.name: Goldsmith I

producer:heroes-guild-t1.description: Guild-era expedition hall that consumes gear, food, keys, luxury goods, and knowledge to return chests and treasure.
producer:heroes-guild-t1.name: Heroes Guild I

producer:house-of-engineers.description: Choose The Path keystone for the Engineers branch. Owning it locks Faith and Mages and prepares later machinery, power, and industrial upgrade lines.
producer:house-of-engineers.name: House of Engineers

producer:house-t1.description: Basic housing that turns warmth and water into early morale.
producer:house-t1.name: House

producer:house-t2.description: Improved housing that consumes processed food and coins to produce stronger morale while still creating lower morale.
producer:house-t2.name: House

producer:house-t3.description: Prosperous housing that consumes clothing, food, and comfort goods to produce mid-tier morale and lower morale.
producer:house-t3.name: House

producer:house-t4.description: Prestige housing that consumes luxury goods to produce the highest morale and all lower morale tiers.
producer:house-t4.name: House

producer:iron-mine-t1.description: Mines iron ore carts from a nearby iron deposit using food and tavern supplies for the crew.
producer:iron-mine-t1.name: Iron Mine I

producer:library-t1.description: Early archive that researches and reissues blueprints from Era I and Era II.
producer:library-t1.name: Library I

producer:library-t2.description: Established library that researches and reissues blueprints up through Era IV.
producer:library-t2.name: Library II

producer:library-t3.description: Advanced library that researches and reissues blueprints up through Era VIII.
producer:library-t3.name: Library III

producer:library-t4.description: Grand library that researches and reissues every currently available blueprint through the final era.
producer:library-t4.name: Library IV

producer:lumberjack-t1.description: Produces logs when placed near a tree.
producer:lumberjack-t1.name: Lumberjack I

producer:mage-lodge.description: Choose The Path keystone for the Mages branch. Owning it locks Engineers and Faith and prepares later rune, mana, and arcane-science lines.
producer:mage-lodge.name: Mage Lodge

producer:market-t1.description: First trade producer that converts processed food and drink into coins.
producer:market-t1.name: Market

producer:market-t2.description: Improved trade producer that expands the market with paper trade and better feast sales.
producer:market-t2.name: Market II

producer:paper-mill-t1.description: Civic workshop that turns planks and water into paper for education and permits.
producer:paper-mill-t1.name: Paper Mill I

producer:pig-farm-t1.description: Consumes grain and water to produce piglets.
producer:pig-farm-t1.name: Pig Farm I

producer:prospector-guild-t1.description: Entry-level prospecting guild that uses civic permits and knowledge to discover clay and sand deposits for advanced construction.
producer:prospector-guild-t1.name: Prospector Guild

producer:prospector-guild-t2.description: Upgraded prospecting guild that keeps surface surveys and adds coal, iron, and gold deposit prospecting for industry.
producer:prospector-guild-t2.name: Prospector Guild

producer:purifier-t1.description: Industrial cleanup producer that must be present near dirty coal or charcoal processing and consumes board pollution together with water and fuel.
producer:purifier-t1.name: Purifier I

producer:quarry-t1.description: Produces stone when placed near a rock.
producer:quarry-t1.name: Quarry I

producer:quarry-t2.description: Upgraded quarry that keeps common stone extraction and adds marble extraction near marble deposits.
producer:quarry-t2.name: Quarry

producer:roof-tile-factory.description: Workshop that fires clay into sturdy roof tiles.
producer:roof-tile-factory.name: Roof Tile Factory

producer:sand-pit.description: Prepared extraction pit that produces sand near a surveyed sand deposit.
producer:sand-pit.name: Sand Pit

producer:sawmill-t1.description: Turns logs into planks when placed near a lumberjack.
producer:sawmill-t1.name: Sawmill I

producer:school.description: Education building that turns civic support into Basic Knowledge.
producer:school.name: School

producer:sheep-pasture-t1.description: Consumes grain and water to produce wool.
producer:sheep-pasture-t1.name: Sheep Pasture I

producer:shrine-t1.description: A small roadside shrine that turns simple offerings into temporary production blessings.
producer:shrine-t1.name: Wayside Shrine

producer:slaughterhouse-t1.description: Processes piglets into sausage and leather.
producer:slaughterhouse-t1.name: Slaughterhouse I

producer:smelter-t1.description: Dirty heavy-industry furnace that refines ore with coal and water into ingots while sometimes dumping board-only pollution.
producer:smelter-t1.name: Smelter I

producer:stonemason-t1.description: Turns stone into stone blocks when placed near a quarry.
producer:stonemason-t1.name: Stonemason I

producer:stonemason-t2.description: Upgraded stonemason workshop that keeps stone block processing and adds polished marble blocks.
producer:stonemason-t2.name: Stonemason

producer:tailor-workshop-t1.description: Clothing producer that sews common and luxury clothing.
producer:tailor-workshop-t1.name: Tailor Workshop I

producer:tannery-t1.description: Leatherworking producer that turns raw hide and water into leather.
producer:tannery-t1.name: Tannery I

producer:tavern-t1.description: Serves beer barrels as individual beers.
producer:tavern-t1.name: Tavern I

producer:townhall-t1.description: Starter civic hub that issues basic settlement blueprints.
producer:townhall-t1.name: Town Hall I

producer:townhall-t2.description: Base food civic hub that issues farming, livestock, garden, and Era III transition plans.
producer:townhall-t2.name: Town Hall II

producer:townhall-t3.description: Food processing and first trade civic hub that issues milling, baking, dairy, cookhouse, brewing, wine, market, and Era IV plans.
producer:townhall-t3.name: Town Hall III

producer:townhall-t4.description: Civic administration hub that issues education, paper, permit, survey, and improved market plans.
producer:townhall-t4.name: Town Hall IV

producer:university.description: Advanced institution that converts paper, glass, coin stacks, and advanced knowledge into Master Knowledge.
producer:university.name: University

effects.label: Faith path locks Engineers and Mages keystone plans
Mages path locks Engineers and Faith keystone plans
Engineers path locks Faith and Mages keystone plans
Mages path locks Engineers and Faith keystone plans
Engineers path locks Faith and Mages keystone plans
Faith path locks Engineers and Mages keystone plans

producer:waste-processor-t1.description: Early cleanup building that slowly recycles trash safely or burns it faster with wood at the cost of pollution risk.
producer:waste-processor-t1.label: I
producer:waste-processor-t1.name: Waste Processor I

producer:waste-processor-t2.description: Upgraded cleanup building that can also burn trash with coal or charcoal for faster disposal and uglier air.
producer:waste-processor-t2.label: II
producer:waste-processor-t2.name: Waste Processor II

producer:weaver-hut-t1.description: Textile producer that weaves wool into common cloth.
producer:weaver-hut-t1.name: Weaver Hut I

producer:winery-t1.description: Produces grapes from water near a vineyard, then presses grapes and water into wine barrels.
producer:winery-t1.name: Winery I
